title: Configure Studiora Free/Premium Monetization with Google Play Billing

requirements.summary: Update useSubscription.ts hook to support Free and Premium tiers with Google Play product IDs, localStorage persistence, and restore functionality
acceptanceCriteria: useSubscription hook exposes isPremium, subscribeToPlan, restorePurchases, cancelSubscription
Premium status is stored and retrieved from localStorage
Product IDs 'studiora_monthly' and 'studiora_yearly' are used
restorePurchases() rehydrates premium state from localStorage on app init

file_operations.description: Refactor useSubscription to define two subscription tiers (Free and Premium) with product IDs 'studiora_monthly' ($3.99/month) and 'studiora_yearly' ($29.99/year). Add localStorage persistence for premium status using keys 'studiora_subscription_plan' and 'studiora_premium_status'. Expose isPremium (boolean), currentPlan ('free' | 'monthly' | 'yearly'), subscribeToPlan(productId: string), restorePurchases(), and cancelSubscription(). The restorePurchases function must check localStorage for existing premium purchases and restore the isPremium flag. Remove existing trial-related logic and replace with simple free/premium distinction.

requirements.summary: Create simulated Google Play Billing integration layer as a standalone utility
acceptanceCriteria: googlePlayBilling.ts exports initBillingClient, launchBillingFlow, acknowledgePurchase, queryPurchases
launchBillingFlow resolves with purchase token on success and rejects on cancellation
queryPurchases checks localStorage for existing premium purchases
Integration is used by the PaywallScreen component

file_operations.description: Create a simulated Google Play Billing integration with Promise-based API matching Google Play Billing's interface. Export functions: initBillingClient() → Promise<void>, launchBillingFlow(productId: 'studiora_monthly' | 'studiora_yearly') → Promise<{ purchaseToken: string; productId: string }>, acknowledgePurchase(purchaseToken: string) → Promise<void>, queryPurchases() → Promise<Array<{ purchaseToken: string; productId: string }>>. On successful purchase simulation in launchBillingFlow, generate a random purchase token, persist to localStorage using key 'studiora_purchases', and resolve. On failure/cancellation, reject with error message 'Payment cancelled or failed.' queryPurchases must read from localStorage key 'studiora_purchases' and return stored purchases array. Include detailed JSDoc comments explaining this is a simulation layer.

requirements.summary: Redesign PaywallScreen to show premium benefits, monthly/yearly plan cards, Google Play CTA, and handle success/failure flows
acceptanceCriteria: PaywallScreen shows title 'Upgrade to Studiora Premium'
Feature list displays all 7 premium benefits with icons
Monthly and Yearly plan cards are both shown; Yearly has a soft 'Best Value' highlight
Prices shown: $3.99/month and $29.99/year
Tapping a plan card selects it and 'Continue with Google Play' triggers billing for that plan
'Restore Purchases' button calls restorePurchases() and shows result feedback
On success: paywall closes and toast shows 'Premium unlocked. Stay focused.'
On failure: inline message 'Payment cancelled or failed.' appears without closing modal

file_operations.description: Redesign PaywallScreen to display: (1) title 'Upgrade to Studiora Premium' at the top, (2) a feature list with icons showing all 7 premium benefits: Unlimited study plans, Advanced analytics, Smart study insights, Advanced focus modes, Detailed statistics, Custom themes, Export reports, (3) two plan selection cards (Monthly $3.99/month and Yearly $29.99/year with 'Best Value' badge softly highlighted in indigo/blue), (4) primary CTA button 'Continue with Google Play' that calls launchBillingFlow from frontend/src/utils/googlePlayBilling.ts with the selected plan's productId, (5) secondary 'Restore Purchases' button that calls restorePurchases() from useSubscription, (6) on successful purchase: call subscribeToPlan with the productId, close the paywall, and display a toast/success message 'Premium unlocked. Stay focused.', (7) on failed purchase: display inline error message 'Payment cancelled or failed.' without closing the modal. Ensure the component uses the SubscriptionContext to access subscribeToPlan and restorePurchases. Remove all trial-related UI.

requirements.summary: Update PremiumGate and UpgradePrompt to show soft, non-blocking upgrade prompt with specific messaging
acceptanceCriteria: PremiumGate shows soft prompt with text 'Available in Premium to enhance your study experience.' for non-premium users
Prompt opens PaywallScreen when tapped
Free features (study plans, tasks, schedule, basic timer, progress summary) remain fully accessible
No hard blocks or forced redirects for free users

file_operations.description: Update PremiumGate to use the isPremium flag from SubscriptionContext (via useConsolidatedPremiumStatus). If isPremium is false, render the UpgradePrompt component with featureName prop. If isPremium is true, render children. Ensure the component does not block navigation or prevent use of free features; it should only show a soft prompt overlay.

Update UpgradePrompt to display the soft upgrade prompt text 'Available in Premium to enhance your study experience.' as the main heading. Add a button labeled 'Learn More' or 'Upgrade' that opens the PaywallScreen modal. Ensure the prompt is non-blocking (overlays the gated content with partial transparency or a subtle card) and does not prevent navigation away. Remove any aggressive or blocking UI patterns. Use Lucide icons to visually represent premium benefits.

requirements.summary: Update SubscriptionContext to incorporate Google Play Billing and auto-restore purchases on app init
acceptanceCriteria: SubscriptionContext exposes isPremium, currentPlan, subscribeToPlan, restorePurchases, cancelSubscription
On provider mount, queryPurchases is called to restore any saved premium status
All premium-gated components consuming the context reflect restored state correctly

file_operations.description: Update SubscriptionContext to wrap useSubscription hook and expose isPremium, currentPlan, subscribeToPlan, restorePurchases, and cancelSubscription. On provider mount (useEffect with empty dependency array), automatically call queryPurchases from frontend/src/utils/googlePlayBilling.ts to check for existing purchases in localStorage, then call restorePurchases() to restore premium state if purchases exist. Ensure all premium-gated components consuming this context reflect the restored state correctly.

requirements.summary: Verify free-tier feature access and ensure premium gates only apply to specified advanced features
acceptanceCriteria: Study plan creation, task management, and daily schedule work fully in free tier
Basic Pomodoro timer works without premium
Progress summary page visible to free users
AdvancedStatisticsSection, AIInsightsPanel, AdvancedFocusSettings, premium themes, and export are gated
Gated features show soft UpgradePrompt, not hard blocks

file_operations.description: Wrap the detailed analytics and bar chart rendering in a PremiumGate component with featureName='Advanced Statistics'. Free users should see a blurred preview and the soft UpgradePrompt overlay. Ensure the component does not block access to the Progress page itself.

Wrap the AI insights rendering in a PremiumGate component with featureName='Smart Study Insights'. Free users should see a preview card with the soft UpgradePrompt overlay. Ensure the component does not block the page.

Wrap the advanced focus settings (custom work/break intervals, ambient sounds, session history) in a PremiumGate component with featureName='Advanced Focus Mode'. Free users should see the soft UpgradePrompt. Basic Pomodoro timer functionality must remain accessible to all users.

Update ThemeSelectorSection to allow free users to select the first 3 theme presets without restriction. For themes beyond the first 3, wrap each premium theme button in a PremiumGate or show a lock icon and trigger the soft UpgradePrompt when tapped. Use isPremium from useConsolidatedPremiumStatus.

Review and remove any premium gate or paywall enforcement for creating the first study plan. Free users must be able to create and manage study plans without restriction. If a paywall was previously enforced for multiple exams, remove it and allow unlimited study plans for all users (the BuildRequest does not restrict study plan count in free tier).

Ensure basic Pomodoro timer (standard work/break intervals) is fully accessible to free users. Do not wrap the timer itself in a PremiumGate. Only wrap advanced settings (AdvancedFocusSettings component) and ambient sound features in premium gates.

Ensure the progress summary (weekly stats, streak, daily progress) is fully visible to free users. Wrap only the AdvancedStatisticsSection and AIInsightsPanel in PremiumGate components. Free users should be able to see their basic progress without any paywall.
